market_overview: High-velocity rotation is currently dominating the Shariah universe. Semiconductor giants are undergoing massive re-ratings following strategic de-risking, while the Data Centre thematic continues to fuel multibillion-ringgit order book expansions in the construction sector.

top_picks.name: INARI
top_picks.analysis: Inari has violently cleared the RM1.80 psychological barrier. The termination of the Lumileds joint acquisition removes a significant earnings overhang, allowing a focused return to core RF and fiber optics excellence.

deep_dive.thesis: Strategic de-risking is the primary driver here. By abandoning the Lumileds deal, Inari preserves its cash pile and avoids a margin-dilutive integration, triggering institutional re-entry.
catalysts: Spike in RF demand for 2026 AI cycles
risks: Immediate profit taking at RM2.15 zone

top_picks.name: IJM
top_picks.analysis: IJM has secured a massive RM658m Hyperscale Data Centre contract from Sime Darby Property. This cements their position as the leading 'digital infra' builder in the region.

deep_dive.thesis: Construction firms with data centre expertise are commanding higher multiples. IJM's rejection of the Sunway takeover confirms that internal value is significantly higher than current market cap.
risks: Margin pressure from raw material inflation

top_picks.name: GBGAQRS
top_picks.analysis: Gabungan AQRS is moving on project handover expectations. As LRT3 nears completion, the release of retention sums will drive a significant improvement in the group's net cash position.

deep_dive.thesis: Classic laggard play trading at a steep discount to NTA. The market is beginning to price in the completion of high-risk legacy jobs.
catalysts: Improved cash flow from project handovers

top_picks.name: FRONTKN
top_picks.analysis: Benefiting from the spillover interest in Inari. Technicals show a steady +2.08% climb on healthy 1.6x volume.

deep_dive.thesis: Frontken provides precision cleaning services for the chip industry and is a lower-beta way to play the semi-conductor recovery.
catalysts: Expansion in Singapore and Taiwan nodes
risks: Sensitivity to global wafer shipment volumes

top_picks.name: 99SMART
top_picks.analysis: 99 Speedmart is defending its support zone. Despite consumer sector headwinds, its high-frequency mini-mart model remains the most resilient retail setup on Bursa.

deep_dive.thesis: Recession-proof growth. The 3,000-store expansion target remains on track, providing high earnings visibility through 2027.

top_picks.name: VITROX
top_picks.analysis: Institutions are rebalancing into high-quality test equipment makers. ViTrox's move into AI-integrated inspection is a long-term thematic winner.

deep_dive.thesis: Quality compounder. While not as explosive as Inari, ViTrox offers lower volatility and exposure to the global ATE recovery.
risks: High P/E valuation sensitive to rate hikes
